text: Face. This astonishment and attention (ver. 5.) intimate the mysteries of the Church, prefigured by the Jewish rites. (Worthington)
Ways, or "the going in," and all that concerns the temple and sacrifices.

Flesh. People of this description were not to enter the temple, which is here called the sanctuary, even though they adored God. In some cases pagans were admitted into any part, except the most holy. Epiphanes (Calmet) and Pompey forced their passage thither, to the great sorrow of the Jews. (Josephus, Jewish Wars 1:5.) See Ecclesiasticus lii., and Acts 21:28.<hr>During the plundering of the city, the Chaldeans did the like; (Lamentations 1:10.; Haydock) and while idols were set up, all rules were neglected.<hr>Doings. You observe the exterior, and not the spirit of religion.
Yourselves, out of ambition, contrary to the regulations of David. (Calmet)

Levites, or priests, (Haydock) who were seduced by Jeroboam, or offered victims on the high places, 4 Kings 23:9. Such lost their privileges and were condemned to serve in the meanest offices. It seems the race of Ithamar was alone guilty, and the threat against his house was now put in execution, 1 Kings 2:39., and 3:14., and Leviticus 21:17., and 2 Esdras 7:64. (Calmet)
Slay. Levites might do this, but the priests poured the blood round the altar, ver. 15. They could not well slay all the victims.
And Levites, of the higher order. (Haydock)<hr>No simple Levites were of this family. It enjoyed the high priesthood form the time of Solomon.
Court; that is to do duty.<hr>Linen. Wool might have been taken from a dead sheep, which would render the person unclean. (Calmet)
Sweat, or so as to make them perspire. Septuagint, "girded violently." (Haydock)<hr>They must have liberty to move, (St. Jerome) and have the girdle about their breast. (Braun.)

Sanctify, by exposing them to the danger of touching the sacred vestments, which none were to touch but they that were sanctified. (Challoner)<hr>Sanctify here means to defile, Exodus 30:29., (Calmet) and 29:37. Holy things must be done in holy places. (Worthington)<hr>Divine religion has different clothing for the ministry, from what is used in common life. (St. Jerome) (Exodus 28:40.) (Calmet)
Shave, like the priests of Isis. (Juvenal vi.)
Wine. Leviticus 10:9. The priests on duty abstain from wine. (Josephus, Jewish Wars 6:15.)
Widow. Moses laid this restraint upon the high priest, Leviticus 21:13. (Calmet)
Unclean, attending the funeral, which inferior priests alone might do, Leviticus 21:10. (Menochius)
Day, after the seven first, (Calmet) though this would be a new regulation. (Menochius)
No. This is omitted in Hebrew, Septuagint, etc. God was their portion. (Calmet)<hr>They had no land but what lay near their cities. (Haydock)
